elements.title: 1261 siege of Mosul
elements.contents: ... that the 1261 siege of Mosul (pictured) occurred after the city's new ruler rejected his father's previous surrender to the Mongols?

elements.title: Liane Moriarty
elements.contents: ... that Liane Moriarty has said that she embraces the labelling of her novels as women's fiction?

elements.title: La Cocina de Doña Esthela
elements.contents: ... that the owner of La Cocina de Doña Esthela initially thought a phone call announcing she received the "world's best breakfast" award was an extortion attempt?

elements.title: Laurie Triefeldt
elements.contents: ... that Laurie Triefeldt taught herself to use a computer program to create World of Wonder to fill the gap between text and illustration?

elements.title: a Russian law professor
elements.contents: ... that a Russian law professor died due to a cocaine balloon bursting in his stomach?

elements.title: Welsh Not
elements.contents: ... that some teachers hung a Welsh Not on the necks of students to discourage them from speaking Welsh?

elements.contents: ... that several members of Iran's women's national team were recently granted asylum in Australia?

elements.title: Geoff Epstein
elements.contents: ... that Geoff Epstein said that one difference between him and Jeffrey Epstein is that "[h]e's a dead American, and I'm an alive Australian"?
